data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-9f20a4bd">
    <title>agent: move claim heartbeat out of beads.jsonl</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently refreshes claimed-bead liveness by rewriting `.ddx/beads.jsonl` every heartbeat interval. In this repo that dirties the git worktree continuously, creates tracker-only commit noise, increases merge conflict risk on the hottest tracker file, and mixes ephemeral worker liveness with durable bead state. FEAT-006 also states the worker/server boundary has no claim lease or heartbeat, so durable tracker heartbeats conflict with the intended execution contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:817-832 starts a heartbeat goroutine for each claimed candidate and calls `w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)` on every ticker interval.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:666-687 implements `Store.Heartbeat` by writing `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` and `updated_at` back into `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:160-164 and 198-206 expose a 30s default worker heartbeat interval, making the tracker rewrite periodic during normal operation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Stop writing periodic liveness heartbeats to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Durable tracker mutations should remain limited to semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, and append-only evidence/events.
- Replace `Store.Heartbeat` usage in the work loop with an ephemeral liveness surface that does not dirty the tracker. Acceptable implementations include an ignored runtime lease file under `.ddx/workers/` or `.ddx/runs/`, server worker state, or another non-tracker runtime store.
- Preserve stale-claim/watchdog behavior by teaching any stale-worker logic to read the new ephemeral liveness surface or, if no reliable runtime heartbeat exists, to rely only on explicit claim metadata plus process/server lifecycle cleanup.
- Update C11/heartbeat extraction expectations if necessary so it extracts or removes the new safe liveness path rather than preserving tracker writes.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing bead claim semantics (`status=in_progress`, owner, claimed-at, claimed-pid).
- Removing append-only execution evidence or semantic bead events.
- Implementing the Axon bead backend; this bug applies to the JSONL tracker path.

DEPS
- Depends on ddx-06eb05d8 because C7 is currently changing the same drain-loop area.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-0b8780db (C11 heartbeat extraction) should depend on this bead so the extraction does not preserve tracker-writing heartbeat behavior.
- ddx-5cb6e6cd execution refactor should not close while periodic `.ddx/beads.jsonl` liveness writes remain in the work loop.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL` verifies a running claimed-bead liveness tick does not change `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes or the bead `updated_at` field.
2. `TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker` verifies the replacement liveness surface is written/read outside `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and is discoverable by worker/watchdog code.
3. `TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite` verifies `Store.Heartbeat` is removed from the work-loop call path or becomes a non-mutating/deprecated no-op with no tracker write.
4. WIRED-IN: `git grep "Store.Heartbeat|.Heartbeat(candidate.ID)|.Heartbeat(beadID)" cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server` shows no periodic worker liveness path writing through the bead JSONL store.
5. C11 (`ddx-0b8780db`) either depends on this bead or its AC is updated to extract the safe liveness path, not the existing tracker-writing heartbeat.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -run "Heartbeat|Liveness|Watchdog|Claim" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, kind:bug, reliability, refactor, spec:FEAT-006, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="95b83bd4756c8a082ff9c21d5561f49b8dfb8324" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Consumer of the Fizeau Execution Contract</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the heartbeat cleanup in the live checkout, but I could not complete the bead because the required full package gate is still red in this workspace.

What changed:
- Removed the obsolete tracker heartbeat field from claim writes in [cli/internal/bead/store.go](/tmp/ddx-verify-ddx-91fe7a1a/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L630).
- Tightened the liveness tests to assert the bead row’s `updated_at` stays stable and that claim liveness is only visible through the external lease file in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go](/tmp/ddx-verify-ddx-91fe7a1a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go#L64).
- Added a guard in the store test to ensure claim liveness is not stored in `beads.jsonl` in [cli/internal/bead/store_test.go](/tmp/ddx-verify-ddx-91fe7a1a/cli/internal/bead/store_test.go#L775).

Verification:
- Passed: `go test ./internal/agent ./internal/bead -run 'Heartbeat|Liveness|Watchdog|Claim' -count=1`
- Failed: `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...`
- The blocking failures are outside the heartbeat change:
  - `TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA`
  - `TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt`
  - `workerprobe` VCS-stamping error from `go build ddx`

I wrote the required evidence note here:
- [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-verify-ddx-91fe7a1a/.ddx/executions/20260507T015406-e88cc31e/no_changes_rationale.txt)

If you want, I can next investigate the unrelated agent/workerprobe failures so the full suite can go green.
